text:
There once was boy who loved bees. He wasn box shirts all day long. One sunny afternoon, he wandered into a field of honeysuckle.

A big bee buzzed toward him. Its eyes twinkled like blackberries. He bobbed just right. They flew together. Birds stared. Owls hooted. They soared gracefully through woods. Eventually, the boy had to go home. That night, dreams filled his head. A bee castle.

He’d go there every night. He built a tiny crown. When dawn arrived, he flew back home.

Sometimes he'd find a flower. Sometimes nothing. Just stars.

He liked stars.

One day, a wise old owl hoodwoven with bees spoke.

“Stars are watchers. Like you.”

“I only watch bees.”

“Ah. You watch time.”

“Then what?”

“Then you watch flowers.”

“I never think of flowers.”

“They dream.”

His dreams would fall open. Flowers. Flowers would talk.

He’d race through
